name: David Lee
bio:
David Lee edits place-based and institutional reports for Artometrics — export identity maps,
city-system comparisons, league economics, and the subliminal architecture of power in creative markets.

His work focuses on making geographic and organizational data legible to readers who care about
culture but do not live inside a spreadsheet. Recent desks include national export atlases,
regional sports identity, and franchise valuation frameworks.

David previously worked in civic data and economic research before joining the Artometrics editorial desk.

name: Emma Carter
bio:
Emma Carter reports on cities, regions, and spatial datasets for the Artometrics Atlas desk — housing
markets, restaurant inspections, bikeshare systems, museum collections, and the micro-economics of place.

She is especially interested in reports where a single metro area becomes a laboratory: San Francisco
versus New York, export superpowers mapped by country, or neighborhood-level inequality read through
public agency tables.

Emma's background spans urban planning and data visualization before joining Artometrics.

name: Isaac Turner
bio:
Isaac Turner edits History desk reports at Artometrics — civilizational cycles, imperial timelines,
long-run economic series, and archival datasets that reward patient reading.

His work emphasizes context over hot takes: when a chart spans centuries, the limitations belong in
the same paragraph as the headline finding.

Isaac has written on Roman emperors, world heritage sites, nuclear testing records, and other sources
where time itself is the primary variable.

name: Jordan Wells
bio:
Jordan Wells covers persona-driven reports at Artometrics — dynasties, founders, creators, and the
behavioral profiles behind institutions that outlast any single leader.

From sports franchises to YouTube channels, Jordan's desk asks who built the system, what incentives
shaped it, and how public data lets readers compare myth to measurable record.

He brings a background in sports analytics and narrative journalism to long-form Artometrics investigations.

name: Juliet Ramos
bio:
Juliet Ramos leads the Culture desk at Artometrics — anime catalogs, streaming libraries, music charts,
film economics, and the business of art across the creative industries.

She specializes in reports that connect audience behavior to market structure: how platforms rank work,
how franchises compound value, and where public datasets reveal what hype obscures.

Juliet writes the bridge between curiosity and evidence — long-form investigations with charts a
non-specialist can actually read.
